releases.headline: Storage Migration, Backup Restore, mTLS Lifecycle, Deploy Hardening
releases.summary: Rollup of v1.7.0 through v1.7.7 covering storage tooling, backup safety, mTLS certificate lifecycle automation, deployment polish, and observability improvements.

items.title: Storage migration between local disk and S3
items.body: Files are copied as raw ciphertext, since the encryption layer is independent of the storage backend, so no re-encryption is required.

items.title: Orphan cleanup tooling
items.body: Finds and removes storage files with no DB record, S3 objects with no DB record, and DB records pointing to missing files.

items.title: Restore from the admin UI
items.body: Pre-restore snapshots of `vault.key`, `db.key.enc`, and `hermitstash.db.enc` are taken for crash recovery before the restore proceeds.

items.title: Encrypted backup manifest
items.body: Backup archives are passphrase-protected and surface a clear error when the supplied passphrase is wrong.

items.title: TLS certificates and mTLS CA included in backup/restore
items.body: Operators no longer need to back these files up out-of-band; they round-trip through the same encrypted archive.

items.title: mTLS client certificate expiry tracking with auto-renewal
items.body: Client certs are renewed automatically before expiry, eliminating the manual reissue chore that previously broke sync connections.

items.title: Client-initiated cert renewal via `POST /sync/renew-cert`
items.body: Both a valid API key and a valid mTLS cert are required; the endpoint returns a fresh client certificate signed by the mTLS CA.

items.title: Admin UI cert reissue for broken sync connections
items.body: Operators can reissue a client cert from the UI when a sync client's existing cert is unrecoverable.

items.title: One-time enrollment codes for sync clients
items.body: Codes such as `HSTASH-A4K9-XMWP-7RB2` bundle the API key and mTLS certs together so the client redeems and gets everything in one round-trip.

items.title: Runtime env vars `PUID`, `PGID`, `UMASK`, `TZ`
items.body: Defaults are `99:100` (Unraid `nobody:users`); override with `1000:1000` for standard Linux. `UMASK` defaults to `022`; use `000` for Unraid sharing.

items.title: Multi-platform install paths
items.body: `docker-compose.yml`, `docker-compose.image.yml`, `docker-compose.coolify.yml`, `kubernetes.yml`, `unraid-template.xml`, plus install scripts for podman, LXD/Incus, Proxmox LXC, Ansible, and Terraform.

items.title: Image tag scheme `:1`, `:1.7`, `:1.7.7`, `:latest`, `:sha-<commit>`
items.body: Operators pick the level of stability they want; user-facing examples now pin to `:1` for minor/patch updates.

items.title: Coolify Docker Compose build pack
items.body: Dedicated `docker-compose.coolify.yml` with `is_directory: true` volumes and a compose-level healthcheck.

items.title: ESLint, Hadolint, and ShellCheck on every push and PR
items.body: Each run posts a summary table comment, and `eslint-plugin-security` adds prompt-injection-style and crypto-pattern rules.

items.title: Migration preview flags missing source files before start
items.body: Operators see how many records have missing source files up front, with a hint pointing to Orphan Cleanup so they do not watch every file fail in turn.

items.title: Per-file errors are visible during and after migration
items.body: The admin UI now exposes per-file detail instead of an opaque `5 failed` count; the same fix lands on restore so path-traversal rejections count toward `failedUploads` rather than being silently dropped.

items.title: Backup scheduler no longer runs on every server restart
items.body: Atomic writes, locking, S3 validation, and safe pruning are in place; individual backups can be deleted from the UI and backup history reloads after a settings save.

items.title: Same-bucket guard for storage and backup
items.body: Storage and backup cannot share the same S3 bucket, which prevents migration into the backup bucket and vice versa.

items.title: Container security hardening
items.body: `init: true` (tini as PID 1), `no-new-privileges`, `cap_drop: ALL` with a minimal `cap_add` set, non-root via `gosu`, and graceful `SIGTERM` shutdown.

items.title: Unraid Community Apps template fixed and consolidated
items.body: The broken `Default="true|false"` syntax is corrected, and the duplicate template at the repo root is consolidated with a self-referencing `TemplateURL`.

items.title: WebSocket heartbeat sent immediately on connect
items.body: Clients confirm the connection is live without waiting for the first 30s interval tick; subsequent heartbeats continue at the existing 30s cadence.

items.title: Silent-failure audit across job and worker code paths
items.body: Every error path that increments a failure counter or skips work now logs at `warn` or `error` with file path, ID, and error message. Affects `storage-migration.service.js`, `restore-worker.js`, `cert-expiry.job.js`, `expiry-cleanup.job.js`, and `chunk-gc.job.js`.

items.title: Slug validator and filename sanitizer rewritten
items.body: Nested-quantifier patterns are replaced with structurally safe regexes, removing the need for linter suppression comments.

items.title: `isS3Path` returns a boolean
items.body: Previously returned `null` / `undefined` for falsy input, which broke callers that did strict comparisons against `true` / `false`.

items.title: `reissueCert` uses `findOne` instead of non-existent `findById`
items.body: The admin-UI reissue path threw on the missing helper; it now resolves the credential record correctly.

items.title: Audit log race condition, path traversal, `var` shadowing, and pre-signed key handling
items.body: Concurrent writes no longer interleave, traversal attempts are rejected, the shadowed binding is renamed, and pre-signed S3 keys are handled correctly.

items.title: Backup history reload after settings save
items.body: The history pane refreshes immediately so a freshly-saved schedule reflects in the visible list without a manual reload.

items.title: S3 storage path stored from `saved.path`
items.body: The S3 backend was previously persisting the local relative path on the DB record; the path now comes from the storage adapter's return value.

items.title: `PUID` / `PGID` ownership check on upgrade from pre-v1.7.2
items.body: Pre-v1.7.2 images ran the container as root; v1.7.x runs as `hermit` and remaps via gosu. Check ownership of `/app/data` and `/app/uploads` after upgrade — the entrypoint chowns them automatically on first start.

items.title: Pin image tag to `:1` or `:1.7.7` instead of `:latest`
items.body: `:1` tracks minor and patch updates within the v1 line; `:1.7.7` pins exactly. Both are preferable to `:latest` for operators who want predictable upgrades.

items.title: Run Orphan Cleanup before Storage Migration
items.body: If migrating local files to S3, run `Orphan Cleanup → Scan` first to surface dangling DB records, clean those up, then run `Storage Migration → Preview → Start`. The preview now flags dangling records inline as a reminder.

items.title: v1.7.6 tag skipped — no Docker image published
items.body: v1.7.6 attempted to pin `gosu=1.17-3+b4` and `curl=8.14.1-2+deb13u2` for Hadolint DL3008 compliance, but the live Debian Trixie mirror versions did not match `node:24-slim`'s apt snapshot. v1.7.7 ships everything from v1.7.6 minus the broken pin.
